text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--- FINAL REPORT ---

PATIENT: Margaret Osei-Bonsu
MRN: [REDACTED]
DATE OF PROCEDURE: [REDACTED]
DATE RECEIVED: [REDACTED]
DATE OF FINAL REPORT: [REDACTED]
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [REDACTED], MD
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED], MD (Gastroenterology/Endoscopy)
CLINICAL SERVICE: Gastroenterology / Hepatobiliary Oncology
CLINICAL INDICATION: 66-year-old female with new-onset worsening diabetes, imaging-identified hypoenhancing mass in the head and uncinate process of the pancreas measuring 3.8 cm; elevated CA 19-9 (1,247 U/mL). EUS-guided fine needle biopsy performed for tissue diagnosis.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN INFORMATION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Specimen ID: [LAB ACCESSION NUMBER REDACTED]
Specimen Type: EUS-guided fine needle biopsy (EUS-FNB), pancreatic head mass
Procedure: Endoscopic ultrasound-guided fine needle biopsy using a 22-gauge Franseen-tip biopsy needle (3 passes performed with fanning technique under real-time EUS guidance). Rapid on-site evaluation (ROSE) performed by cytotechnologist at time of procedure; preliminary assessment: malignant cells present, adequate cellularity.
Material Submitted:
  - Jar A: Core biopsy tissue fragments in formalin (2 cores, pale tan-white, aggregate 0.9 cm in length)
  - Jar B: Cell block preparation from needle rinse
  - Jar C: Diff-Quik-stained smears (cytology, reviewed separately under Cytopathology accession)

Note: Cytology smear results reported separately (see concurrent cytopathology report). The current report pertains to the formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core biopsy tissue and cell block material only.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and medical record number, are two tan-white, slightly firm, cylindrical core biopsy fragments measuring 0.6 cm and 0.3 cm in greatest dimension, respectively. The tissue is somewhat pale and fibrotic in texture with no grossly identifiable hemorrhage or necrosis. Both cores are submitted entirely, wrapped in lens paper, and processed in a single cassette (Block A1). Additionally received is a cell block preparation labeled "pancreatic head FNB needle rinse," containing a tan-white micropellet of approximately 0.2 cm in greatest dimension, submitted in a single cassette (Block A2). Representative sections submitted as follows:
  - Block A1: Core biopsy tissue, 2 fragments, entirely submitted
  - Block A2: Cell block preparation, entirely submitted

Total tissue submitted: 2 cassettes.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Microscopic examination of the formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded core biopsy material reveals multiple histologic sections of pancreatic parenchyma infiltrated by an invasive epithelial neoplasm. The sections are of adequate diagnostic quality with minimal crush artifact.

The tumor is characterized by irregular, angulated, and haphazardly arranged glandular structures composed of cuboidal to columnar epithelial cells with moderate to abundant amphophilic cytoplasm. Nuclear pleomorphism is moderate: nuclei are enlarged, vesicular, and irregular with variably prominent nucleoli. Nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratios are increased. Mitotic figures are identified, including occasional atypical mitoses. Intraluminal mucin is present in a subset of glandular profiles. The tumor glands show incomplete luminal formation and focal cribriforming in areas. Infiltration is irregular and jagged at the advancing front, with individual tumor cells and small abortive glands extending into the surrounding fibrous stroma.

Background stroma is markedly desmoplastic, composed of dense, hypocellular collagenous fibrous tissue with scattered activated stellate cells and a sparse chronic inflammatory infiltrate predominantly composed of lymphocytes and macrophages. This desmoplastic reaction is a prominent and characteristic feature, constituting approximately 60-70% of the total tissue volume on the core biopsy sections.

No unequivocal perineural invasion is identified in the available sections; however, assessment is limited given the small sample size. Lymphovascular invasion is not identified in the tissue provided. No necrosis is identified.

Residual non-neoplastic pancreatic acini are present at the periphery of the core material and show reactive/atrophic changes, including loss of zymogen granules and lobular atrophy, consistent with obstructive pancreatitis. Islets of Langerhans are focally identified. No pancreatic intraepithelial neoplasia (PanIN) is definitively identified in the limited sample, though focal ductal epithelial changes are present and cannot be fully characterized on biopsy.

The cell block (Block A2) shows similar findings with small clusters of malignant glandular epithelium embedded in fibrous stroma, confirming the core biopsy findings.

Histologic grade: Moderately differentiated (Grade 2 of 3) based on glandular architecture and degree of nuclear pleomorphism.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (performed on Block A1):
A panel of immunohistochemical stains was performed to confirm pancreatic origin, exclude alternative primary sites, and assess relevant prognostic/predictive markers. Results are as follows:

  CK7 (cytokeratin 7):         POSITIVE -- diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells
  CK20 (cytokeratin 20):       NEGATIVE -- no staining in tumor cells (internal positive control: colonic mucosa on control slide reactive)
  CDX2:                        NEGATIVE -- no nuclear staining in tumor cells (excludes lower GI primary)
  MUC1 (EMA):                  POSITIVE -- diffuse apical and cytoplasmic membrane staining in tumor cells
  MUC2:                        NEGATIVE -- consistent with pancreatic ductal rather than mucinous/goblet cell phenotype
  MUC5AC:                      POSITIVE (focal, weak) -- nonspecific, can be seen in PDAC
  SMAD4 (DPC4, clone EP618Y):  LOSS OF EXPRESSION (0/4+ nuclear staining) -- complete absence of nuclear immunoreactivity in tumor cells; retained nuclear expression in background stromal and endothelial cells (positive internal control). This pattern is consistent with biallelic inactivation of SMAD4 (homozygous deletion or biallelic inactivating mutation).
  p53:                         OVEREXPRESSION (diffuse, strong nuclear positivity, 3+, >80% of tumor nuclei) -- aberrant pattern consistent with gain-of-function TP53 mutation
  Ki-67 (MIB-1):               Proliferation index approximately 35% (moderately high; hot spots counted)
  CA19-9 (by IHC):             POSITIVE -- focal cytoplasmic staining in tumor cells
  Synaptophysin:               NEGATIVE -- excludes neuroendocrine differentiation
  Chromogranin A:              NEGATIVE
  SOX17:                       Not performed
  HER2:                        EQUIVOCAL (1+ incomplete membrane staining) -- insufficient for amplification assessment on biopsy; further testing may be considered if clinically indicated (FISH or NGS-based copy number assessment)

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY INTERPRETATION:
The immunophenotype (CK7+/CK20-/CDX2-/MUC1+/MUC2-) is consistent with pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma and does not support a lower gastrointestinal, upper gastrointestinal, or biliary primary site as an alternative. SMAD4 loss of expression by IHC is a well-recognized finding in approximately 55% of PDACs and indicates biallelic SMAD4 inactivation, which may have prognostic significance. Aberrant p53 overexpression is consistent with a missense TP53 gain-of-function mutation. These findings together support a primary pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

MISMATCH REPAIR (MMR) PROTEIN IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY: PENDING
  MLH1 -- PENDING
  PMS2 -- PENDING
  MSH2 -- PENDING
  MSH6 -- PENDING
  [Note: MMR IHC ordered as reflexive protocol for all newly diagnosed pancreatic adenocarcinomas; results to be issued as an addendum to this report upon completion. If MMR deficiency is identified, reflex microsatellite instability (MSI) PCR testing will be performed.]

PD-L1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (22C3 pharmDx, Dako): PENDING
  [Note: PD-L1 ordered per institutional protocol; combined positive score (CPS) to be reported in addendum.]

MOLECULAR / GENOMIC TESTING:
  Comprehensive genomic profiling (CGP) by next-generation sequencing (324-gene panel): Material from this block (Block A1) has been submitted for NGS-based comprehensive genomic profiling. Results to be reported separately under molecular pathology accession. Specimen adequacy for molecular testing was confirmed (estimated tumor cellularity approximately 30-35% on the section submitted for extraction).

  KRAS mutation by PCR/pyrosequencing (reflexive): PENDING (to be superseded by NGS results)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
FINAL PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PANCREAS, HEAD AND UNCINATE PROCESS, EUS-GUIDED FINE NEEDLE BIOPSY:

  PANCREATIC DUCTAL ADENOCARCINOMA (INFILTRATING DUCT CARCINOMA, NOS)
  -- Histologic grade: Moderately differentiated (Grade 2 of 3)
  -- Prominent desmoplastic stromal reaction
  -- SMAD4 (DPC4) loss of expression by immunohistochemistry (see ancillary studies)
  -- Aberrant p53 overexpression by immunohistochemistry, consistent with TP53 missense mutation
  -- Ki-67 proliferation index: approximately 35%
  -- Perineural invasion: not identified in sections examined (limited assessment on biopsy)
  -- Lymphovascular invasion: not identified
  -- MMR immunohistochemistry: PENDING (addendum to follow)
  -- PD-L1 (CPS): PENDING (addendum to follow)
  -- Molecular/NGS testing: submitted; results to be reported separately

Note: Correlation with clinical and imaging findings is recommended. The morphologic and immunophenotypic features are consistent with a primary pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. The tumor involves the head and uncinate process as described radiographically. No assessment of surgical margins, lymph nodes, or extent of spread can be made on this biopsy specimen.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
SYNOPTIC COMMENT
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

This moderately differentiated pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma shows the typical constellation of morphologic features, including infiltrative glandular architecture, desmoplastic stroma, and moderate nuclear atypia. The immunohistochemical demonstration of complete SMAD4 protein loss (consistent with homozygous deletion or biallelic inactivating mutation) is noteworthy, occurring in approximately 55% of PDACs, and has been associated in some series with a higher propensity for distant hematogenous metastasis. The aberrant p53 overexpression pattern is consistent with a missense TP53 mutation and is a near-ubiquitous finding in PDAC. Pending molecular studies (comprehensive NGS panel) will further characterize the genomic landscape of this tumor and may identify actionable alterations or confirm germline testing recommendations.

This report has been reviewed and signed out by the attending pathologist of record.

[Signed electronically by Attending Pathologist -- signature on file]

ADDENDUM (to be issued upon completion of pending studies):
  - MMR IHC results
  - PD-L1 CPS (22C3)
  - KRAS reflexive PCR result (if applicable prior to NGS)

--- END OF REPORT ---
